route.name: Pacific Coast Highway: California Section

sources.title: Cycling FKT - Pacific Coast Highway
sources.notes: Public source page for the Canada-to-Mexico PCH FKT route; its GPX link points to the RideWithGPS route.

sources.title: RideWithGPS - PACIFIC COAST FKT FINAL FULL
sources.notes: Public route page lists 1,657.5 mi and 79,943 ft for the whole preferred PCH route; direct GPX export is account-gated.

sources.title: Public Google Drive - Pacific Coast Cycling Route
sources.notes: Public folder containing PACIFIC CYCLE ROAD.gpx; used as the downloadable GPX source and clipped to California.

sources.title: Adventure Cycling - Pacific Coast Route
sources.notes: Reference source for the broader Pacific Coast cycling corridor; not used as a downloadable GPX source.

route.sourceNote: Planning package geometry resolved from routes/pacific-coast-highway-california-section.gpx. California-only GPX derived by clipping the public PACIFIC CYCLE ROAD.gpx whole-Pacific-coast source at the first trackpoint south of 42.0 N; no artificial routing was added.
route.description: PCH / Full California coastal corridor from the Oregon border area to San Diego, derived from a public whole-Pacific-coast GPX source and clipped to the California section.
route.surfaceMix: Mostly paved coastal touring route; road stress, detours, weather, and logistics matter more than technical trail difficulty.
route.bestSeason: Broad-season coastal route outside major winter storm damage, fire/smoke periods, holiday traffic spikes, and extreme wind/fog windows.

resources.water: Frequent coastal services in many sections, but remote northern and Big Sur/Central Coast stretches need explicit water spacing review.
resources.food: Resupply is generally available in coastal towns, but hours and long-gap planning need verification for the full corridor.
resources.camping: Requires a multi-week California coast campground/lodging sequence across Del Norte, Humboldt, Mendocino, Sonoma/Marin, Bay Area, Central Coast, Los Angeles/Orange, and San Diego counties.
resources.toiletsShowers: Confirm by campground/service source before riding.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest bike shops before gate confirmation.
resources.longestGap: Measure from resolved geometry before gate confirmation.

tips: Keep Plan Ready until the full California corridor is broken into rideable itinerary chunks with current Caltrans/park closures, overnight reservations, water/service gaps, traffic/shoulder stress, and bailout plan verified.
hazards: Full-corridor Highway 1/US-101 traffic, shoulder, bridge, and construction-detour review required.
Exact multi-week campground/lodging and reservation sequence unresolved.
Current Caltrans, Big Sur slide, county road, park, fire, smoke, and storm-damage checks not recorded.
Water/service gap and bailout audit required before gate-confirmed use.
Source GPX is a public whole-Pacific-coast file clipped to California; route-line QA is still needed before navigation.
